reforms.description: Mexico has made significant progress in easing procedures to start a business through the launch of the Sistema de Apertura Rápida de Empresas (SARE), which has streamlined registration processes for low-risk businesses. Additionally, the government plans to integrate SARE with tuempresa.gob.mx, an electronic one-stop shop to ease compliance with federal business start-up regulations, significantly reducing the time required for registration.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Streamlining business registration processes encourages entrepreneurship and formalization, which can enhance productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has announced plans for the integration of SARE with tuempresa.gob.mx.
reforms.importance_rationale: The integration of SARE with tuempresa.gob.mx represents a significant but narrower reform aimed at improving the business environment.
reforms.source_quote: Mexico has made significant progress in easing procedures to start a business, including through the launch in 2002 of the Sistema de Apertura Rápida de Empresas (SARE).

reforms.description: The regulatory review 'Base Cero' was initiated to update federal regulations, aiming to simplify processes and enhance productivity while facilitating technology absorption to strengthen innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Simplifying regulations can enhance productivity and facilitate technology absorption, which supports innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The regulatory review has been initiated and is in force.
reforms.importance_rationale: The 'Base Cero' initiative represents a structural change aimed at improving the regulatory environment for innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The objective is to improve and simplify regulations to favour productivity and facilitate technology absorption, so as to strengthen innovation as a basis of economic growth.

reforms.description: Mexico has developed the Mexico Emprende program, which provides web-based information and business centers to assist micro, small, and medium-sized enterprises in enhancing their productivity and formalizing their operations.
reforms.package_name: Support for Micro and Small Enterprises
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Providing resources and support to micro and small enterprises can enhance their productivity and contribute to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The program has been developed and is operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: The Mexico Emprende program represents a significant initiative to support small businesses, though it is not as comprehensive as major reforms.
reforms.source_quote: Mexico has developed these kinds of services through its programme Mexico Emprende with web-based information and a number of business centres that attend micro as well as small and medium-sized enterprises throughout the country.

reforms.description: A recent labor reform proposal in Mexico aims to limit the accumulation of due wages during labor trials, which could reduce the costs and uncertainties associated with hiring and firing, potentially encouraging formal employment.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Reducing hiring and firing costs can encourage formal employment, which supports economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The labor reform proposal has been announced but not yet legislated.
reforms.importance_rationale: The labor reform proposal represents a significant change aimed at improving labor market conditions.
reforms.source_quote: The reform is still under discussion in Congress, but if it were implemented along the lines of the original proposal, this would ease the burden of employment protection legislation quite substantially.
